page.content: The data presented in the table provides a comparative analysis of outcomes related to patient mortality one month post-intervention, segmented by field group classification. The analysis distinguishes between two patient cohorts: a non-shrinking field group (N = 47) and a shrinking field group (N = 50).

page.content: Notably, the table indicates that 29 patients (44%) in the non-shrinking field group experienced mortality within one month, contrasted with 37 patients (56%) from the shrinking field group. The grade distribution of the observed cases further elucidates the severity of conditions affecting these patients, with grade classifications indicating clinical outcomes.

page.content: The calculated P value serves to assess the statistical significance of the differences noted between the two groups, thereby providing insights into the implications of field group classification on patient survival rates. This analysis is critical for understanding the relationship between cystic characteristics and patient prognosis in clinical settings.
